explanatory_strength.rationale: The liquidation NAV bridge cleanly separates real-estate NRV, cash, loans payable, and the cost accrual, and the dated payoff scenarios name the Article 78 and B2K financing checkpoints.

evidence_sufficiency.rationale: Audited liquidation-basis statements anchor the claim, but the three disclosed debt facilities still do not fully reconcile to the $10.79M balance-sheet total and the October 2026 Rialto maturity is not stress-tested with explicit refinance or dilution math.

downside_control.rationale: The modeled bear case still annualizes above 13% per year, yet FY2025 already cut disclosed NAV about 15%, the component low case leaves only about 9% upside to price, and management's possible additional capital through 2028 is not quantified in the stress path.

return_vs_alternatives.rationale: Base annualized return clears a credit hurdle if the 2028 liquidation schedule holds, but refinancing risk, a 15% mortgage facility, and an unresolved litigation overhang keep the realized range closer to the low-case 3% annualized path than the headline base case.

claims.claim: Loans payable of about $10.8M are senior to equity distributions and the October 2026 Rialto mortgage maturity is a near-term refinancing checkpoint while a separate facility was recently refinanced at 15%.

Management's disclosed liquidation NAV already embeds debt and cost accruals, but FY2025 showed a material downward re-estimation and management states additional capital may be needed through 2028, so equity recovery can shrink even if collateral value is real.

Component overlap controls appear clean and the embedded B2K option is not double-counted, but the credit cross-check cannot sign off until the debt waterfall and maturity schedule reconcile to the audited liability lines.

strongest_counter_explanation: The headline $11.79 liquidation NAV assumes optimistic B2K pricing and an on-schedule 2028 finish; an adverse Article 78 ruling, failed October 2026 refinance, or dilutive capital raise could compress equity recovery toward the $6.30 component low case while fixed claims stay senior.
most_important_missing_fact: Line-item reconciliation of the three disclosed loan facilities to the $10,790,194 balance-sheet total, including rates, covenants, and the expected outcome of the October 2026 Rialto mortgage maturity.
falsifiers: Reconcile each loan facility to the audited loans-payable total with maturity schedule and covenant headroom.
Model a dilutive capital raise through 2028 and its per-share impact on the liquidation payoff.
Stress a failed or punitive refinance at the October 2026 maturity against cash, rental receipts, and remaining asset-sale timing.
specialist_findings: Credit cross-check: leverage and fixed claims are material but subordinate to real-estate NRV; survivability looks plausible if refinances extend on acceptable terms, yet the vote stays on watch until debt reconciliation and the October 2026 checkpoint are closed.
